A cramped, low-ceilinged basement courtroom beneath a larger legislative hall in an unfinished stone building, Washington D.C., 1803. Bare plaster walls, rough wooden benches, and a modest elevated bench where a tall lean white man in his late 40s in a black judicial robe and white cravat presides. Several robed associate justices sit beside him. A small group of men in dark wool frock coats and white cravats stand before the bench holding rolled parchment documents. A few observers crowd the narrow gallery. Oil lamps and candles cast warm uneven light across the austere chamber. Mood is tense and expectant.
